title: sida inclusa
intro: Sida inclusa, also known as the prickly apple or horseweed, is a perennial plant species native to tropical Africa and parts of Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Sida inclusa include its potential to treat various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as its use as an antiseptic and astringent. Sida inclusa can be prepared as a herbal remedy in various forms, including teas, infusions, and poultices, which are made by boiling the leaves or roots of the plant in water.
